title: Hemigraphis decipiens
taxonomy: Hemigraphis decipiens is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is commonly known as the "silver shield plant" or "blue dragon plant". It is native to tropical Asia and is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and indoor spaces. The classification of Hemigraphis decipiens is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, and clade - Asterids. It is a member of the genus Hemigraphis, which contains various species of flowering plants.

intro: Hemigraphis decipiens, commonly known as the "False Thai Basil" or "Indian Veldt Basil", is a species of flowering plant in the Acanthaceae family, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemigraphis decipiens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
